Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details

Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)

dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia


Now I have everything needed to produce the cross-suite summary.



TL;DR: T-GM cnfdf32 ts2phc stuck in FREERUN (clock class 248) cascades to 30 failures; 5 more from a pmc command regression; 2 from a new T-BC suite lacking matching lab hardware.



Root Cause Groups:


• 30/38 failures — T-GM ts2phc FREERUN on cnfdf32 (clock class stuck at 248, cascading to all BC/follower/slave sync tests)

Affects: tgm (10), dualnicbc (8), dualnicbcha (8), dualfollower (4).

DPLL metric shows LOCKED (=1) but ts2phc metric stays FREERUN (=0). Root mechanism: NoSourceTSCount == 2 in pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go:211-212 forces ts2phcState = PTP_FREERUN in event.go:405-407, preventing the GM state machine from issuing clock class 6. Without class 6: ptp4l stays at 248, phc2sys never starts (gated in daemon.go:1284-1293), BC slave interfaces stay or fall back to MASTER role, and all downstream sync tests time out. Breaking commit unconfirmed — a cnfdf32 GNSS antenna/hardware fault cannot be ruled out as an alternative explanation.


• 5/38 failures — pmc rejects GET PARENT_DATA_SET with "bad command"

Affects: dualnicbc (3: "Slave can sync to master", "Downstream slave can sync to BC master", "Can provide a profile with higher priority"), dualnicbcha (2: same first two).

Both NICs (/var/run/ptp4l.0.config, /var/run/ptp4l.1.config) produce bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. This is not a "no parent" error (which would imply sync failure); pmc is actively rejecting the command syntax, indicating a version or invocation-format regression in the test binary's pmc helper, independent of GM state.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC suite [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts: no WPC NIC topology found in lab

Affects: tbc (1 serial test + BeforeSuite entry).

PR #205 (aede1e93, merged 2026-06-23 on main) introduced PtpConfigTelcoBC() and the new tbc suite. The L2 graph solver requires a WPC NIC (E810/E830 with GNSS/PPS) with 3 co-located interfaces plus a WPC GM on the same LAN. Lab nodes cnfdf30/cnfdf31/cnfdf32 do not expose this hardware profile, so solutions["TBC"] is empty and BeforeSuite aborts. Deterministic, reproduces on every run.


• 1/38 failures — ts2phc restart counter reset bug in cleanup.go

Affects: tgm ("Verify ts2phc metrics are cleaned up after GM PtpConfig delete and re-apply").

Counter resets from 7 to 4 on config re-apply instead of continuing to accumulate. Isolated bug in cleanup logic; independent of the FREERUN condition.



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causetgmdualnicbcdualnicbchadualfollowertbc
GM ts2phc FREERUN (cascade)10884—
pmc "bad command" regression—32——
T-BC hardware missing————2
Counter-reset bug1————

• The FREERUN cascade is cross-suite and cross-node: tgm tests it directly; dualnicbc and dualnicbcha fail because their BC nodes (cnfdf30/cnfdf31) cannot upstream-sync to a FREERUN GM; dualfollower on cnfdf31 cannot lock because its upstream path is broken.
• The pmc regression is dualnicbc/dualnicbcha-specific — only tests that call pmc GET PARENT_DATA_SET are affected.
• The T-BC failure is tbc-suite-specific and fires before any test runs.
• The counter-reset bug is tgm-suite-specific and is the only failure not attributable to GM FREERUN within tgm.


Actionable Recommendations:


1. Investigate cnfdf32 GNSS/ts2phc immediately (highest ROI — unblocks 30 failures): Check whether cnfdf32 suffered a GNSS antenna disconnect, signal loss, or hardware fault during this run. Inspect ts2phc logs for "source ts not valid" lines and the NoSourceTSCount accumulation. Also audit recent changes to pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go and event.go:405-407 on main for any behavior change that would trigger FREERUN prematurely even when DPLL is locked (DPLL=1 but ts2phc=0 is the key diagnostic).

2. Audit and fix the pmc command invocation in test helpers (unblocks 5 failures): Reproduce the bad command: GET\nbad command: PARENT_DATA_SET failure locally against the current pmc binary version in the CI image. Compare against the expected invocation format and check for a pmc version upgrade in the linuxptp-daemon image that changed the command interface (e.g., pmc -u -b 0 'GET PARENT_DATA_SET' vs. pmc -u -b 0 GET PARENT_DATA_SET).

3. Gate the tbc suite on WPC NIC hardware availability (unblocks 2 failures per run): Add a BeforeSuite hardware-capability check that skips or marks the suite as Pending when no WPC NIC satisfying the T-BC topology constraints is detected, rather than fatally failing. Alternatively, provision the lab with a node that has the required hardware before enabling this suite in the periodic job.


Overall Issue Class: infra_other (dominant cause — GM hardware/GNSS fault on cnfdf32 drives 30/38 failures; cannot confirm a code regression as the trigger without hardware telemetry) with a secondary test_regression component (T-BC suite added without matching lab hardware, pmc invocation regression).


Overall Severity: CRITICAL — 79% of tests fail due to a single infrastructure node (cnfdf32) being unable to achieve GNSS lock; the entire PTP timing chain for the lab is broken, making all BC/follower/slave sync testing impossible until cnfdf32 is restored.




Per-Suite Details



tbc



Suite: tbc (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The only failing TBC test cannot even start because [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] fails immediately — the L2 topology solver finds zero interfaces satisfying the T-BC hardware discovery constraints (WPC NIC + 3 co-located interfaces + local GM on same LAN).


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-BC hardware topology discovery returns no solutions (affects: [It] [tbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Reboot discovery The slave node is rebooted and discovered and in sync [Serial])
• Evidence:

```

Test: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

Message: Could not create a ptp config

Expected

<*errors.errorString | 0xc0007ac770>:

no T-BC solution found for TBC

{

s: "no T-BC solution found for TBC",

}

to be nil

```

```

Test: [It] [tbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Reboot discovery The slave node is rebooted and discovered and in sync [Serial]

Message: Could not create a ptp config, err=no T-BC solution found for TBC

```

• Root Cause: PtpConfigTelcoBC() in test/pkg/testconfig/testconfig.go:1898-1900 returns fmt.Errorf("no T-BC solution found for %s", BestSolution) when len(*data.solutions["TBC"]) == 0, meaning the L2 graph solver found no set of interfaces satisfying the T-BC algorithm constraints: a WPC NIC receiver (StepIsWPCNic at line 705), two transmitters on the same NIC as the receiver (StepSameNic at lines 706-707), and a local GM on the same LAN that is also a WPC NIC (lines 708-709). IsWPCNicWrapper in vendor/github.com/redhat-cne/l2discovery-lib/pkg/graphsolver/graphsolver.go:380-405 requires an interface with a valid PHC index, and either (WPC subsystem ID + PTP pins) or (PTP pins + GNSS device) — if the lab worker nodes (cnfdf30/cnfdf31/cnfdf32) lack that hardware topology, the solutions slice stays empty and BeforeSuite aborts all tbc tests.
• Breaking PR/Commit: PR #205 (aede1e9 — merged main June 23 2026, "T-BC tests") introduced the tbc test suite and PtpConfigTelcoBC(). Not backported to release-5.0 (production images are unaffected; this is a test-binary regression). Test binary runs from main (TEST_BRANCH=main), so this commit is the source of the new suite running against a lab that has not been verified to provide a valid T-BC hardware topology.
• Is it a flake? No — the error is deterministic topology-discovery failure at BeforeSuite; it will reproduce on every run against this cluster unless the lab hardware or discovery logic is fixed.
• Suggested Fix: Verify that at least one worker node in the CI lab has a WPC NIC (Intel E810/E830 with PTP pins + GNSS device exposed) with three usable interfaces (one slave + two masters) on the same PHC, plus a second WPC NIC on the same L2 LAN to act as local GM; if the required hardware is not present, the tbc suite must be gated (skipped) on that cluster profile, or the lab hardware must be provisioned accordingly.

Suite Issue Class: test_failure

Suite Severity: MEDIUM


all



Suite: all (1 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] for the TBC job aborted before any tests could run because the L2-topology solver found zero valid interface assignments satisfying the T-BC WPC NIC constraints on the lab nodes.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-BC solver returns no solution for lab hardware (affects: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]: Could not create a ptp config)
• Evidence:

```

Test: [SynchronizedBeforeSuite]

Message: Could not create a ptp config

Expected

<*errors.errorString | 0xc0007ac770>:

no T-BC solution found for TBC

{

s: "no T-BC solution found for TBC",

}

to be nil

```

Also from junit-failures.txt:

```

Test: [It] [tbc-serial] PTP e2e tests PTP Reboot discovery The slave node is rebooted and discovered and in sync [Serial]

Message: Could not create a ptp config, err=no T-BC solution found for TBC

```

• Root Cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite calls testconfig.CreatePtpConfigurationsWithRetry(3) → createPtpConfigurations() → PtpConfigTelcoBC(isExtGM) (testconfig.go:607). PtpConfigTelcoBC (testconfig.go:1891–1899) selects BestSolution = AlgoTelcoBCString ("TBC") and immediately returns fmt.Errorf("no T-BC solution found for %s", BestSolution) when len(*data.solutions["TBC"]) == 0. The solver requires the T-BC receiver to be on a WPC (E810 GNSS/PPS) NIC (StepIsWPCNic, testconfig.go:705), with two transmitters on the same WPC NIC (lines 706–707), and a local grandmaster also on a WPC NIC (lines 708–709). The lab worker nodes (cnfdf30–cnfdf32, all bare x86_64 workers) do not expose WPC NIC capabilities in L2 discovery, so the solver produces zero solutions. The test code runs from main (TEST_BRANCH=main per build log), where PR #205 (aede1e93, merged 2026-06-23) introduced the PtpConfigTelcoBC function and the AlgoTelcoBCString solver problem definition for the first time. The branch-awareness rule does not apply here: the failing code is test infrastructure on main (not a release-5.0 image), so it is directly in effect in this job without needing a cherry-pick.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/openshift/ptp-operator/pull/205 — commit aede1e93dd5a86adc0f45b8513c87600a84ff18b ("Merge pull request #205 from nocturnalastro/tbc_ci_tests", merged 2026-06-23 on main). Commit 84723c83 ("test: Add T-BC CI tests and configuration") is the actual implementation. Note: check_backport.py could not be run (scripts/check_backport.py not present locally), but this is a test-code change running from main so backport status to release-5.0 is not required for it to be the root cause.
• Is it a flake? No — the solver deterministically finds zero WPC NIC solutions on the current lab topology. This will reproduce on every run until either the lab hardware exposes WPC NIC capabilities to L2 discovery or the solver constraints are relaxed.
• Suggested Fix: Either provision a lab node with an Intel E810 GNSS/PPS WPC NIC whose capabilities are visible to L2 discovery, so the StepIsWPCNic constraint (testconfig.go:705,709) can be satisfied; or, if the TBC job is intended for hardware that does not have WPC NICs, relax the AlgoTelcoBCString problem definition in testconfig.go to reflect the actual lab topology (e.g., remove the StepIsWPCNic requirement and use a standard NIC T-BC path).

Suite Issue Class: test_regression

Suite Severity: HIGH


tgm



Suite: tgm (11 failures)


Suite TL;DR: The T-GM node (cnfdf32) never achieves ts2phc LOCKED state (clock class 248 instead of 6), blocking phc2sys startup and event generation across all 11 tests; one additional test exposes a deterministic counter-reset bug in cleanup.go.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: T-GM ts2phc stuck in FREERUN — GM clock class never reaches 6 (affects: "Should recover clockClass via event API after cloud-event-proxy crash", "WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs", "WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on metrics", "Verify ts2phc clock state is not LOCKED after GM PtpConfig is deleted", "WPC GM GNSS signal loss tests Testing WPC T-GM holdover through connection loss", "WPC GM ts2phc termination tests Continuously terminating ts2phc triggers FREERUN event and then recovers to LOCKED", "WPC GM Events verification (V2) Testing T-GM Events on GNSS reboot and recovery")
• Evidence:

```

openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens7fx",node="cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com",process="dpll"} 1

openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens7fx",node="cnfdf32.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com",process="ts2phc"} 0

Expected ptp4l clock class to eventually be 6 for GM

Expected : false to be true

Expected phc2sys to be 1 for GM

Expected : false to be true

```

• Root Cause: The DPLL on cnfdf32 is LOCKED (metric=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (metric=0). In pkg/event/event.go:405-407, when parser.NoSourceTSCount == 2 (set in pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go:211-212 on repeated "source ts not valid" log lines), ts2phcState is forced to PTP_FREERUN regardless of actual DPLL state. With ts2phc FREERUN, the GM state machine (updateGMState in event.go:369) cannot set clock class 6, so ptp4l stays at class 248. phc2sys startup is gated in daemon.go:1284-1293 on the PHC achieving synchronization — since ptp4l never locks, phc2sys remains DOWN (status=0). The same pattern is seen identically in dualnicbc and dualnicbcha suites on the same GM node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Unknown — linuxptp-daemon is built from main (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git). The nearest visible commit is 2de8784 (upstream-sync 2026-07-15, merged 2026-07-16, after this job ran). Cannot confirm a specific PR without full git history. Possible hardware GNSS interruption on cnfdf32 is an alternative explanation that cannot be ruled out.
• Is it a flake? No — the failure is consistent across tgm, dualnicbc, and dualnicbcha suites all targeting the same GM node, indicating a systemic condition rather than a timing fluke.
• Suggested Fix: Check if recent changes to NoSourceTSCount handling in pkg/parser/ts2phc_parser.go or event.go:405-407 incorrectly force ts2phc to FREERUN; also inspect whether cnfdf32 had a GNSS antenna/hardware fault during this run. The NoSourceTSCount global is package-level state that is not reset on PtpConfig re-apply, which may cause stale FREERUN forcing across test cases.

• Group 2: Log stream context deadline exceeded on event-disabled containers (affects: "Should check for ptp events", "Should receive events after linuxptp-daemon pod restart", "WPC GM Verification Tests is verifying WPC GM state based on logs")
• Evidence:

```

PTP event was not generated in the pod linuxptp-daemon-hqqxg, err=could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-hqqxg container=cloud-event-proxy, err=error reading from stream: context deadline exceeded

error reading logs in ns=ptp-event-framework-test pod=consumer: context deadline exceeded

could not find regex in log stream for openshift-ptp/linuxptp-daemon-2mzms container=linuxptp-daemon-conta

2026-07-15T00:01
❌ failure5.05.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream257mtest_regression CRITICAL
linuxptp-daemon `main` PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.
38 failed tests
INFRA LOW
🆕 New issue
Full AI Analysis

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)

dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)

Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia

Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.

1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

```go

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"
