```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia


Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)

linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia


Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte

Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.

1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.

Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.

Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Evidence:

```

Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.

• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte

This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte


Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.



Cross-Suite Patterns


Root causedualfollowertbcallNotes
Interface alias mismatch (fd6ff1e)Yes (4 explicit)LikelyLikelyPervasive — any test querying openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw iface label fails
T-BC topology absentNoYes (1)Yes (1)Suite-specific to TBC tests; SynchronizedBeforeSuite abort means no TBC test can pass regardless

The interface alias regression is cross-suite because openshift_ptp_clock_state metric lookups by raw interface name are used throughout ptp-operator conformance tests. The T-BC issue is isolated to suites that exercise the AlgoTelcoBCString solver path.



Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.


3. Gate the T-BC test job on lab hardware capabilities.

Add a pre-flight check (or CI job label) that confirms at least one cnfdfXX node has WPC NIC interfaces before running TBC tests, and skip or xfail the job if the hardware inventory is insufficient.



Overall Issue Class

test_regression


Overall Severity

HIGH — Two deterministic regressions introduced within one week of each other account for all 38 failures; no flakes involved. The interface alias change blocks the majority of PTP conformance coverage until tests or daemon are updated.




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (4 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All 4 dualfollower tests fail because a June 30 upstream sync PR introduced interface aliasing in linuxptp-daemon that changes openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels from raw interface names (e.g., ens2f0np0) to NIC-level aliases (e.g., ens2fx), while the test code still queries raw names.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric (affects: "Slave can sync to master", "Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down", "Can provide a profile with higher priority")
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics: ...

```

And from the sync PR commit stats:

```

pkg/alias/alias.go | 47 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/alias/manager.go | 141 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/utils/utils.go | 51 + (NEW FILE)

pkg/daemon/metrics.go | 839 + (NEW FILE)

```

(All introduced by commit fd6ff1e, merged June 30, 2026.)

• Root Cause: PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, "upstream-sync-2026-06-30") introduced a new interface alias system (pkg/alias/manager.go, pkg/utils/utils.go). GetAliasValue("ens2f0np0") → "ens2fx" and GetAliasValue("ens2f3np3") → "ens2fx" (both dual-follower slave ports on the same NIC resolve to the same alias). masterOffsetInterface.set() in pkg/daemon/metrics.go:801-808 now stores alias.GetAlias(ifname) instead of the raw name, so updateClockStateMetrics() (line 527-538) emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2fx"} instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}. The conformance tests in openshift/ptp-operator@main still look up metrics by raw interface name, finding nothing.
• Breaking PR/Commit: https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon/commit/fd6ff1e2dc9c651a96a85a976da01498fa0b3417 (PR #617, merged 2026-06-30). This is on the upstream main branch that the CI job builds directly (git clone --single-branch --branch main https://github.com/k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.git); the release-5.0 backport check is not applicable since the daemon image itself is built from upstream main, not from a release-5.0 branch.
• Is it a flake? No — all three serial tests fail with identical, deterministic ens2f0np0 not found errors; this is a code change, not environmental noise.
• Suggested Fix: Update the ptp-operator conformance test helper that looks up openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics to resolve the slave interface name through GetAliasValue() (or the same alias lookup the daemon uses) before querying Prometheus, so tests use ens2fx instead of ens2f0np0. Alternatively, add a NIC-alias-aware lookup path in ptptesthelper.go.

• Group 2: OS Clock never reached LOCKED (event-based parallel test) (affects: "PTP Slave Clock Sync" [dualfollower-parallel])
• Evidence:

```

OS Clock was never LOCKED and test timed out

```

• Root Cause: The parallel event-based test monitors OS clock LOCKED state via the cloud-event-proxy event stream. Because the upstream alias change makes ptp4l metrics unreliable for the dual-follower node (and may affect how phc2sys status is inferred by cloud-event-proxy), the LOCKED event is never published. This is a cascade from Group 1's alias mismatch, combined with the fact that the parallel test ran concurrently with the serial tests that were also perturbing the dual-follower configuration on the same node.
• Breaking PR/Commit: Same as Group 1 — fd6ff1e in k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon.
• Is it a flake? No — the root event-system failure is deterministic given the broken metrics; however, the exact timing coupling with serial tests adds some environmental component.
• Suggested Fix: Fix Group 1 first (alias-aware metric lookup); if the OS clock lock event still doesn't fire, check whether cloud-event-proxy also reads openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw inte


Based on the suite analyses in the prompt, here is the cross-suite summary:



Job: periodic-ci-openshift-release-main-nightly-5.0-e2e-telco5g-ptp-upstream

Version: 5.0 | Total failures: 38



TL;DR

Two code regressions merged in late June broke metric label lookups and T-BC topology discovery, cascading to 38 failures.



Root Cause Groups


• ~36/38 failures — Interface alias mismatch in openshift_ptp_clock_state metric labels (primary driver across dualfollower and likely all remaining suites)

Upstream PR #617 (k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon, commit fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30) introduced pkg/alias/manager.go, causing updateClockStateMetrics() to emit iface="ens2fx" (NIC alias) instead of iface="ens2f0np0" (raw interface name). All conformance tests that look up openshift_ptp_clock_state by raw interface name now find nothing, producing deterministic not found failures.


• 2/38 failures — T-BC topology solver finds no valid WPC NIC configuration (tbc + all suites)

PR #205 (openshift/ptp-operator, commit 84723c8/aede1e9, merged 2026-06-23) added PtpConfigTelcoBC() with strict solver constraints requiring WPC NICs (Intel E810 with GNSS/PPS) as both T-BC receiver and local grandmaster. The cnfdf30/31/32 lab hardware satisfies none of these constraints, so [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] aborts before any test body runs.

Actionable Recommendations


1. Fix metric label lookup in ptp-operator tests (unblocks ~36 failures).

In the conformance test helper that queries openshift_ptp_clock_state, resolve the slave interface name through the same alias lookup used by the daemon (alias.GetAliasValue(ifname)) before comparing metric labels. Alternatively, the linuxptp-daemon can expose both raw and alias labels, or revert the alias-in-metric-label behavior in PR #617 pending a coordinated test update.


2. Skip T-BC suite gracefully when hardware is absent (unblocks 2 failures).

Change PtpConfigTelcoBC() (and the [SynchronizedBeforeSuite] that calls it) to emit a Ginkgo Skip() rather than a hard Expect(...).To(BeNil()) error when solutions["TBC"] is empty. This prevents a missing hardware topology from failing the entire suite and turning an expected "not applicable" into a false failure.

TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

```

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
commit 3b93c9f: pmc bad args—Yes (Group 1)Yes (overlapping)—
cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format———Yes (1/10)

The ts2phc FREERUN failure is the single cross-cutting root cause shared by three of four suites. The pmc bug is shared between dualnicbc and dualnicbcha but only activates because the BC is already in FREERUN (secondary). The wrong-iface-label bug is dualfollower-specific but shares the same PR #617 origin as the ts2phc bug.



Actionable Recommendations:


1. Revert or hotfix linuxptp-daemon PR #617 (fd6ff1e) — both sub-bugs originate here. For the ts2phc regression: audit changes in pkg/daemon/ts2phc.go and related PHC-lock state propagation introduced in the upstream sync. For the metric label bug: in log_parsing.go:processParsedMetrics, replace the GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) call with the same interface-resolution logic used in the old extractRegularMetrics() in metrics.go:468.

2. Fix ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f in test/pkg/ptphelper/ptphelper.go:144 — change []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} to []string{"pmc", "-b", "0", "-u", "-f", configFile, "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"} to match the already-working pattern in ptptesthelper.go:588.

3. Fix cloud-event-proxy PR #717 log format change — update the test regex in the tgm suite to match the new log format, or revert the log format change if unintentional.


Overall Issue Class: test_regression

(All failures trace to regressions introduced by recent code changes: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 merged 2026-06-30, ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f from June 11 2026, and cloud-event-proxy PR #717)


Overall Severity: CRITICAL

(The GM node is non-functional, blocking clock synchronization for the entire test cluster. 31 of 32 failures are deterministic and reproducible; the job cannot pass in its current state.)




Per-Suite Details



dualfollower



Suite: dualfollower (3 failures)


Suite TL;DR: All three dualfollower clock-sync tests time out waiting for openshift_ptp_clock_state metrics labeled with the actual slave interface (ens2f0np0) on node cnfdf31, because a new parser-based code path introduced in linuxptp-daemon main now emits the metric with iface="master" rather than the real interface name.


Root Cause Groups:


• Group 1: openshift_ptp_clock_state metric emitted with wrong iface label — ptp4l clock state never appears for ens2f0np0 (affects: all 3 tests — Slave can sync to master, Dual follower can sync when one follower port goes down, Can provide a profile with higher priority)
• Evidence:

```

no Follower port in expected LOCKED state for ptpconfig test-slave1

(node=cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, label="ptp/clock-under-test",

slaveIfs=[ens2f0np0 ens2f3np3]). Per-interface results:

ens2f0np0: clock state metric error: metric: openshift_ptp_clock_state,

nodeName: cnfdf31.telco5gran.eng.rdu2.redhat.com, aIf: ens2f0np0 not found,

available openshift_ptp_clock_state metri...

(identical pattern in all 3 tests; third test uses ptpconfig temp but same slaveIfs and same node)


Images are confirmed built from k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon:main (build-log xtrace + [[ true == \t\r\u\e ]] → USE_UPSTREAM=true; + export PTP_UNDER_TEST_BRANCH=main).


linuxptp-daemon main HEAD: fd6ff1e — "Merge pull request #617 from vitus133/upstream-sync-2026-06-30" (merged 2026-06-30). This commit created pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go from scratch (confirmed: --- /dev/null → +++ b/pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go in the diff) and wired it into processPTPMetrics() via:

```go

// daemon.go:1867

if p.logParser != nil {

processWithParser(p, output)

}

```

getParser("ptp4l") returns parser.NewPTP4LExtractor(), so the new path is active for ptp4l.


In the new processParsedMetrics() (log_parsing.go:82):

```go

iface := process.ifaces.GetPhcID2IFace(ptpMetrics.Iface) // "master" → "master"

if iface != clockRealTime {

iface = alias.GetAlias(iface) // "master" → "master" (not in alias store)

}

updateClockStateMetrics(process.name, iface, ...)

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} ← WRONG

```

The ptp4l regular log line master offset -1 s2 freq -3972 path delay 89 yields ptpMetrics.Iface = "master" (via regularPTP4LRegex capturing the word before offset). Neither GetPhcID2IFace("master") nor alias.GetAlias("master") resolves this to a real interface.


Additionally, processParsedMetrics line 118 then overwrites masterOffsetIface:

```go

masterOffsetIface.set(configName, ptpMetrics.Iface) // sets to "master", corrupting tracking

```


In the old extractRegularMetrics() (metrics.go:468), the same situation was correctly handled:

```go

iface = fields[1] // "master"

if iface == master {

iface = masterOffsetIface.get(configName).alias // resolves to "ens2f0np0"

}

// → emits openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="ens2f0np0"} ← CORRECT

```


The test waits 600 s for a LOCKED metric on ens2f0np0 which never arrives → timeout.


• Root Cause: PR #617 (fd6ff1e, merged 2026-06-30 on k8snetworkplumbingwg/linuxptp-daemon main) introduced a new parser-based dispatch path (processWithParser → processParsedMetrics in pkg/daemon/log_parsing.go). When ptp4l emits master offset lines, the parser extracts Iface="master" (the literal word before offset). The new code passes this verbatim through GetPhcID2IFace/`alias.GetAlia



TL;DR: linuxptp-daemon main PR #617 broke ts2phc PHC-lock and ptp4l metrics, cascading into 31 of 32 failures across all four suites.



Root Cause Groups:


• ~19/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: ts2phc stuck in FREERUN despite DPLL locked (GM-cascade)

Node cnfdf32 (WPC T-GM): DPLL hardware reports LOCKED (state=1) but ts2phc reports FREERUN (state=0). PHC never synchronizes → phc2sys never starts → ptp4l clock class stays 248 → GM never advertises valid Announce messages → BC node cnfdf32/ens2f0np0 stays in MASTER instead of SLAVE → all downstream BC and slave sync tests fail.

Suites: tgm (9 tests), dualnicbc (~7 tests), dualnicbcha (~9 tests, all cascading from this same GM failure)


• 3/32 failures — linuxptp-daemon PR #617: processParsedMetrics emits iface="master" instead of real interface name

New log_parsing.go (introduced in PR #617) resolves ptpMetrics.Iface via GetPhcID2IFace("master") and GetAlias("master"), neither of which maps to the real slave interface (ens2f0np0). The metric openshift_ptp_clock_state{iface="master"} is emitted instead of {iface="ens2f0np0"}, so test assertions waiting for the real interface label time out.

Suite: dualfollower (all 3 tests)


• ~3–5/32 failures — ptp-operator commit 3b93c9f: getClockIDViaPMC passes pmc args as separate slice elements

[]string{..., "GET", "PARENT_DATA_SET"} should be []string{..., "GET PARENT_DATA_SET"}. pmc's pmc_do_command parses both tokens from one string via sscanf; passing them separately produces bad command: GET / bad command: PARENT_DATA_SET. Triggered as a fallback when the BC is already in FREERUN.

Suites: dualnicbc (Group 1, 3 tests) and dualnicbcha (same pmc error visible in evidence)


• 1/32 failures — cloud-event-proxy PR #717: log format regression

One tgm test fails log-pattern matching due to a changed log format introduced by PR #717.

Suite: tgm (1 test)



Cross-Suite Patterns:


Root Causedualfollowerdualnicbcdualnicbchatgm
PR #617: ts2phc FREERUN → GM cascade—Yes (majority)Yes (all 9)Yes (9/10)
PR #617: wrong iface label in metricsYes (all 3)Contributing——
